top_categories.workout_context: 62 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
56ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tour de Yabakei's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
At Tour de Yabakei, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Tour de Yabakei. Train it until it feels boring.
Tour de Yabakei's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 62 miles.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
Base fitness for Tour de Yabakei. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Low-cadence force work for 3,500ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.

race_overlay.heat: May in Nakatsu, Oita, Japan can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Tour de Yabakei. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Tour de Yabakei’s 62 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Tour de Yabakei. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 62 miles of mixed terrain in Nakatsu, Oita, Japan.
